revision.text:
[[de:Cheri-cheped-Kenmut]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = W12:D21-Aa1*p:D46-n:V31-X1 G14-N14-N33According to the writing in the Sethos I text of the Nutbuch. The use of the hieroglyph W12 instead of T28 deviates from the usual spelling. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qid=Q447064|1=Ancient Egyptian cryptography}} of the Nutbuch, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME2 = T28:D21 K1:N35 V31:N35 X1 G14 N14According to the writing in the Coffin Texts of the Middle Kingdom; see Christian Leitz: ''LGG - Vol 6: H̱ - s -'', Peeters, Leuven 2002, p. 39. |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Cheri-cheped-en-Kenmut
''Ḫr-3-ḫpd-n-knm.t''
''The one under the rear of the Kenmut animal'' }} '''Cheri-cheped-Kenmut''' was the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} designation of a {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which as a {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qid=Q804527}} comprised only the {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} {{ill|Denebola|en|Denebola|qid=Q13015|lt=Denebola|1=Denebola}}. == Background == In the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|qid=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}}, Cheri-cheped-Kenmut appears in the {{ill|Decan lists (Book of Nut)|de|Dekanlisten (Nutbuch)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan lists (Book of Nut)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at various positions and represented the third {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}} on {{ill|star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|lt=Diagonal star clock|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/star clock|qid=Q142353|1=Star clock}} depictions in the Coffin Texts during the reign of {{ill|Amenemhat I|en|Amenemhat I|qid=Q18771|lt=Amenemhat I|1=Amenemhat I}}. In the seventh year of {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}}, a reordering of the decan list was initiated, placing Cheri-cheped-Kenmut in the second position. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|qid=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was dated to the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=16th Shemu I|de|16. Schemu I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}July 25 or 31 (the observation date depends on the reference location).. The leading role of the first Baktiu was assumed by {{ill|Kenmut (Decan)|de|Kenmut (Dekan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut (Decan)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, whose heliacal rising occurred on the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Shemu I|de|6. Schemu I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}July 15 or 21 (the observation date depends on the reference location).. == Representation == Cheri-cheped-Kenmut is depicted {{ill|Iconography|en|Iconography|qid=Q208145|lt=Iconography|1=Iconography}}ally either as a lion-headed {{ill|Sekhmet|en|Sekhmet|qid=Q146104|lt=Sekhmet|1=Sekhmet}} with a cobra on her head or as a falcon-headed {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}. Another association is made with the deity "the one with the Horus children." As a decan deity, Cheri-cheped-Kenmut was responsible for the second hour in the {{ill|Book of the Night|de|Buch von der Nacht|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Night|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. He was particularly revered during the {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman period|qid=Q105747718|1=Greco-Roman Egypt}} in {{ill|Buto|en|Buto|qid=Q739278|lt=Buto|1=Buto}} and was included in the offering ritual there. == See also == * {{ill|List of Decan stars|de|Liste der Dekan-Sterne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Decan stars|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|lt=LGG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qid=Q979923|1=Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}} - Vol 6: H̱ - s -'', Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8 * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5 == References == {{translated page|de|Cheri-cheped-Kenmut|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:ケリ・チェペド・ケンムト]] [[de:Cheri-cheped-Kenmut]] {{wikidata link|Q972923}}
